003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.commons.logging;
018
019import static java.util.logging.Level.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
021
022import java.util.*;
023import java.util.concurrent.*;
024import java.util.logging.*;
025
026/**
027 * Extended logger that provides convenience methods for logging formatted messages.
028 *
029 * <p>
030 * This class extends {@link java.util.logging.Logger} and uses delegation internally to wrap
031 * an underlying {@link java.util.logging.Logger} instance. This design provides:
032 * </p>
033 * <ul>
034 *    <li>Type compatibility - extends Logger for use anywhere a Logger is expected
035 *    <li>Flexibility - can wrap existing logger instances
036 *    <li>Convenience methods for formatted logging using {@link org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts#fs(String, Object...)}
037 * </ul>
038 *
039 * <h5 class='section'>Features:</h5>
040 * <ul>
041 *    <li>All standard {@link java.util.logging.Logger} functionality via delegation
042 *    <li>Convenience methods for formatted logging at all log levels
043 *    <li>Lazy evaluation of formatted messages using {@link org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts#fs(String, Object...)}
044 *    <li>Support for both MessageFormat-style and printf-style formatting
045 * </ul>
046 *
047 * <h5 class='section'>Format Support:</h5>
048 * <p>
049 * The formatting methods support both MessageFormat-style and printf-style formatting:
050 * </p>
051 * <ul>
052 *    <li><b>Printf-style:</b> <js>"%s"</js>, <js>"%d"</js>, <js>"%.2f"</js>, etc.
053 *    <li><b>MessageFormat-style:</b> <js>"{0}"</js>, <js>"{1,number}"</js>, etc.
054 *    <li><b>Un-numbered MessageFormat:</b> <js>"{}"</js> - Sequential placeholders
055 * </ul>
056 *
057 * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
058 * <p class='bjava'>
059 *    Logger <jv>logger</jv> = Logger.getLogger(MyClass.<jk>class</jk>.getName());
060 *
061 *    <jc>// Formatted logging at different levels</jc>
062 *    <jv>logger</jv>.severe(<js>"Error processing user {0}: {1}"</js>, userId, error);
063 *    <jv>logger</jv>.warning(<js>"Failed to connect to %s after %d attempts"</js>, hostname, attempts);
064 *    <jv>logger</jv>.info(<js>"Processing {} items"</js>, count);
065 *    <jv>logger</jv>.fine(<js>"Debug value: %s"</js>, debugValue);
066 * </p>
067 *
068 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
069 *    <li class='jc'>{@link org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts#fs(String, Object...)}
070 *    <li class='jc'>{@link org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.StringUtils#format(String, Object...)}
071 *    <li class='jc'>{@link java.util.logging.Logger}
072 * </ul>
073 */
074@SuppressWarnings({
075   "java:S100",  // Method names match java.util.logging.Logger for API compatibility
076   "java:S1192", // String literals intentionally duplicated for clarity
077   "java:S2176"  // Class name intentionally matches java.util.logging.Logger; extends it to provide Juneau-specific logging enhancements
078})
079public class Logger extends java.util.logging.Logger {
080
081   /**
082    * Permanent registry of logger instances by name.
083    *
084    * <p>
085    * Uses an unbounded {@link ConcurrentHashMap} so every name maps to exactly one
086    * {@link Logger} instance for the JVM lifetime.  An evictable cache (e.g. one with a
087    * {@code maxSize} that clears the whole map) would cause a class-loaded
088    * {@code static final Logger} field to refer to an instance that is no longer in the
089    * registry; a subsequent call to {@link #getLogger(String)} would then return a new,
090    * different instance, breaking {@link LogRecordCapture} and any other code that
091    * assumes one name == one identity.
092    */
093   private static final ConcurrentHashMap<String, Logger> loggers = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();
094
095   /**
096    * The underlying logger instance that we delegate to.
097    */
098   private final java.util.logging.Logger delegate;
099
100   /**
101    * List of log record listeners.
102    */
103   private final List<LogRecordListener> listeners = Collections.synchronizedList(new ArrayList<>());
104
105   /**
106    * Protected constructor - wraps an existing logger instance.
107    *
108    * @param delegate The underlying logger to delegate to.
109    */
110   protected Logger(java.util.logging.Logger delegate) {
111      super(delegate.getName(), null);
112      this.delegate = delegate;
113   }
114
115   /**
116    * Creates a logger for the specified name.
117    *
118    * <p>
119    * This method returns the same Logger instance for a given name, ensuring that
120    * listeners attached to a logger persist across multiple calls to {@link #getLogger(String)}.
121    * Logger instances are cached and automatically created using the underlying
122    * {@link java.util.logging.Logger#getLogger(String)}.
123    *
124    * @param name The logger name.
125    * @return A logger instance (cached and reused for the same name).
126    */
127   public static Logger getLogger(String name) {
128      return loggers.computeIfAbsent(name, k -> new Logger(java.util.logging.Logger.getLogger(k)));
129   }
130
131   /**
132    * Creates a logger for the specified class.
133    *
134    * @param clazz The class.
135    * @return A logger instance.
136    */
137   public static Logger getLogger(Class<?> clazz) {
138      return getLogger(cn(clazz));
139   }
140
141   /**
142    * Creates a log record capture for testing.
143    *
144    * <p>
145    * This method creates a {@link LogRecordCapture} that will capture all log records
146    * from this logger. The capture should be closed when done (typically using try-with-resources).
147    *
148    * @return A LogRecordCapture instance.
149    */
150   @SuppressWarnings({
151      "resource" // Caller takes ownership of the returned LogRecordCapture
152   })
153   public LogRecordCapture captureEvents() {
154      return new LogRecordCapture(this);
155   }
156
157   /**
158    * Adds a log record listener.
159    *
160    * @param listener The listener to add.
161    */
162   void addLogRecordListener(LogRecordListener listener) {
163      listeners.add(listener);
164   }
165
166   /**
167    * Removes a log record listener.
168    *
169    * @param listener The listener to remove.
170    */
171   void removeLogRecordListener(LogRecordListener listener) {
172      listeners.remove(listener);
173   }
174
175   /**
176    * Central logging method that all logging calls feed through.
177    *
178    * <p>
179    * This method creates a {@link LogRecord}, notifies all listeners, and then delegates
180    * to the underlying logger.
181    *
182    * <p>
183    * The method will proceed even if the log level is not loggable if there are listeners
184    * attached, allowing listeners to capture all log records regardless of level.
185    *
186    * @param level The log level.
187    * @param msg The log message or format pattern.
188    * @param args The format arguments, or <jk>null</jk> if none.
189    * @param thrown The throwable, or <jk>null</jk> if none.
190    */
191   private void doLog(Level level, String msg, Object[] args, Throwable thrown) {
192      if (!isLoggable(level) && listeners.isEmpty())
193         return;
194
195      // Create LogRecord with lazy formatting support
196      var rec = new LogRecord(getName(), level, msg, args, thrown);
197
198      // Notify all listeners
199      listeners.forEach(x -> x.onLogRecord(rec));
200
201      // Delegate to underlying logger (LogRecord extends java.util.logging.LogRecord)
202      delegate.log(rec);
203   }
204
205   // Convenience methods with formatted strings
206
207   // Standard Logger methods - feed through central doLog method
208   @Override
209   public void severe(String msg) {
210      doLog(SEVERE, msg, null, null);
211   }
212
213   @Override
214   public void warning(String msg) {
215      doLog(WARNING, msg, null, null);
216   }
217
218   @Override
219   public void info(String msg) {
220      doLog(INFO, msg, null, null);
221   }
222
223   @Override
224   public void config(String msg) {
225      doLog(CONFIG, msg, null, null);
226   }
227
228   @Override
229   public void fine(String msg) {
230      doLog(FINE, msg, null, null);
231   }
232
233   @Override
234   public void finer(String msg) {
235      doLog(FINER, msg, null, null);
236   }
237
238   @Override
239   public void finest(String msg) {
240      doLog(FINEST, msg, null, null);
241   }
242
243   @Override
244   public void log(Level level, String msg) {
245      doLog(level, msg, null, null);
246   }
247
248   @Override
249   public void log(Level level, String msg, Object param1) {
250      doLog(level, msg, new Object[]{param1}, null);
251   }
252
253   @Override
254   public void log(Level level, String msg, Object[] params) {
255      doLog(level, msg, params, null);
256   }
257
258   @Override
259   public void log(Level level, String msg, Throwable thrown) {
260      doLog(level, msg, null, thrown);
261   }
262
263   @Override
264   public boolean isLoggable(Level level) {
265      return delegate.isLoggable(level);
266   }
267
268   @Override
269   public Level getLevel() {
270      return delegate.getLevel();
271   }
272
273   @Override
274   public void setLevel(Level newLevel) {
275      delegate.setLevel(newLevel);
276   }
277
278   @Override
279   public String getName() {
280      return delegate.getName();
281   }
282
283   @Override
284   public void addHandler(Handler handler) {
285      delegate.addHandler(handler);
286   }
287
288   @Override
289   public void removeHandler(Handler handler) {
290      delegate.removeHandler(handler);
291   }
292
293   @Override
294   public Handler[] getHandlers() {
295      return delegate.getHandlers();
296   }
297
298   // Convenience methods with formatted strings
299
300   /**
301    * Logs a SEVERE level message with formatted string.
302    *
303    * @param pattern The format pattern.
304    * @param args The format arguments.
305    */
306   public void severe(String pattern, Object...args) {
307      doLog(SEVERE, pattern, args, null);
308   }
309
310   /**
311    * Logs a SEVERE level message with formatted string and throwable.
312    *
313    * @param thrown The throwable.
314    * @param pattern The format pattern.
315    * @param args The format arguments.
316    */
317   public void severe(Throwable thrown, String pattern, Object...args) {
318      doLog(SEVERE, pattern, args, thrown);
319   }
320
321   /**
322    * Logs a WARNING level message with formatted string.
323    *
324    * @param pattern The format pattern.
325    * @param args The format arguments.
326    */
327   public void warning(String pattern, Object...args) {
328      doLog(WARNING, pattern, args, null);
329   }
330
331   /**
332    * Logs a WARNING level message with formatted string and throwable.
333    *
334    * @param thrown The throwable.
335    * @param pattern The format pattern.
336    * @param args The format arguments.
337    */
338   public void warning(Throwable thrown, String pattern, Object...args) {
339      doLog(WARNING, pattern, args, thrown);
340   }
341
342   /**
343    * Logs an INFO level message with formatted string.
344    *
345    * @param pattern The format pattern.
346    * @param args The format arguments.
347    */
348   public void info(String pattern, Object...args) {
349      doLog(INFO, pattern, args, null);
350   }
351
352   /**
353    * Logs an INFO level message with formatted string and throwable.
354    *
355    * @param thrown The throwable.
356    * @param pattern The format pattern.
357    * @param args The format arguments.
358    */
359   public void info(Throwable thrown, String pattern, Object...args) {
360      doLog(INFO, pattern, args, thrown);
361   }
362
363   /**
364    * Logs a CONFIG level message with formatted string.
365    *
366    * @param pattern The format pattern.
367    * @param args The format arguments.
368    */
369   public void config(String pattern, Object...args) {
370      doLog(CONFIG, pattern, args, null);
371   }
372
373   /**
374    * Logs a CONFIG level message with formatted string and throwable.
375    *
376    * @param thrown The throwable.
377    * @param pattern The format pattern.
378    * @param args The format arguments.
379    */
380   public void config(Throwable thrown, String pattern, Object...args) {
381      doLog(CONFIG, pattern, args, thrown);
382   }
383
384   /**
385    * Logs a FINE level message with formatted string.
386    *
387    * @param pattern The format pattern.
388    * @param args The format arguments.
389    */
390   public void fine(String pattern, Object...args) {
391      doLog(FINE, pattern, args, null);
392   }
393
394   /**
395    * Logs a FINE level message with formatted string and throwable.
396    *
397    * @param thrown The throwable.
398    * @param pattern The format pattern.
399    * @param args The format arguments.
400    */
401   public void fine(Throwable thrown, String pattern, Object...args) {
402      doLog(FINE, pattern, args, thrown);
403   }
404
405   /**
406    * Logs a FINER level message with formatted string.
407    *
408    * @param pattern The format pattern.
409    * @param args The format arguments.
410    */
411   public void finer(String pattern, Object...args) {
412      doLog(FINER, pattern, args, null);
413   }
414
415   /**
416    * Logs a FINER level message with formatted string and throwable.
417    *
418    * @param thrown The throwable.
419    * @param pattern The format pattern.
420    * @param args The format arguments.
421    */
422   public void finer(Throwable thrown, String pattern, Object...args) {
423      doLog(FINER, pattern, args, thrown);
424   }
425
426   /**
427    * Logs a FINEST level message with formatted string.
428    *
429    * @param pattern The format pattern.
430    * @param args The format arguments.
431    */
432   public void finest(String pattern, Object...args) {
433      doLog(FINEST, pattern, args, null);
434   }
435
436   /**
437    * Logs a FINEST level message with formatted string and throwable.
438    *
439    * @param thrown The throwable.
440    * @param pattern The format pattern.
441    * @param args The format arguments.
442    */
443   public void finest(Throwable thrown, String pattern, Object...args) {
444      doLog(FINEST, pattern, args, thrown);
445   }
446
447   /**
448    * Logs a message at the specified level with formatted string.
449    *
450    * @param level The log level.
451    * @param pattern The format pattern.
452    * @param args The format arguments.
453    */
454   public void logf(Level level, String pattern, Object...args) {
455      doLog(level, pattern, args, null);
456   }
457
458   /**
459    * Logs a message at the specified level with formatted string and throwable.
460    *
461    * @param level The log level.
462    * @param pattern The format pattern.
463    * @param thrown The throwable.
464    * @param args The format arguments.
465    */
466   public void logf(Level level, String pattern, Throwable thrown, Object...args) {
467      doLog(level, pattern, args, thrown);
468   }
469}
